tldr: All 4 live ads are exact duplicates running two near-identical 'New Arrivals' messages — 2 are in danger, 2 are warming. Expect CPM lift of 15–25% within 7–10 days unless the rotation diversifies.

benchmark.context: Luxury womenswear peers typically rotate 6–10 distinct angles per season. Roland Mouret is running 2 messages across 4 placements, putting fatigue 13 days ahead of category median.

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
drivers: Exact duplicate of Ad #2 — frequency stacks across the same audience
Generic 'New Arrivals' headline gives the algorithm no segmentation signal
'Elegance made easy' is category-table-stakes copy with no product or silhouette anchor

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
drivers: Identical twin to Ad #1 — splits learning, doubles served frequency
No mention of the Galaxy dress or any hero piece to differentiate from Ad #1
'Day to night' positioning competes directly with the eveningwear ads, cannibalizing intent

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
drivers: Duplicates Ad #4 word-for-word — frequency ceiling approaching
'Reimagined' is unsupported by visual or product specificity in copy
Missing price anchor, silhouette name, or occasion (gala, black-tie, wedding guest)

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
drivers: Twin of Ad #3 — Meta will throttle one or both within 2 weeks
Same headline 'New Arrivals' used across all 4 ads erases relevance signal
No social proof, editorial mention, or stockist credibility

ad_to_scale.headline: New Arrivals
ad_to_scale.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
ad_to_scale.why: The eveningwear angle is more specific than 'day to night' and historically converts at higher AOV for Roland Mouret given the Galaxy heritage. Consolidate spend onto Ad #3, pause its duplicate Ad #4, and let it carry the eveningwear lane while new concepts ship.

ad_to_kill.headline: New Arrivals
ad_to_kill.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
ad_to_kill.why: It's a literal duplicate of Ad #1 with no creative or audience differentiation, so it's only inflating frequency on the same buyers. Killing it reclaims budget for fresh angles before the danger-tier ads tip CPMs.

concepts.hook: The dress that built the house.
concepts.primary_text: Twenty years on, the Galaxy still does what no other dress can — sculpt, hold, transform. Cut from double-faced crepe and built on the bias seam Roland made famous, it remains the silhouette every other label measures itself against. Discover the SS26 Galaxy edit, in three new shades made for the season ahead.
concepts.visual_direction: Slow 360° on the Galaxy dress against a stone backdrop, intercut with archival runway footage and a close-up of the seam construction.
concepts.fills_gap: Replaces vague 'New Arrivals' with a hero-product anchor — gives the algorithm a clear intent signal.

concepts.primary_text: Every Roland Mouret piece begins on the stand, not the screen. From the first toile to the final hand-finish, our SS26 eveningwear takes up to eight weeks to make — pattern-cut in London, sewn in Europe, fitted on a real woman before a single piece ships. This is what tailoring looks like when nothing is rushed.
concepts.visual_direction: Carousel walks through pattern-cutting, draping on stand, hand-stitching, final fitting, finished gown on model, detail shot.
concepts.fills_gap: Adds craft credibility and format diversity (carousel) — current rotation is 100% single-image.

concepts.primary_text: From the Cannes red carpet to a Tuscan rehearsal dinner, Roland Mouret is the dress chosen for the moments that get photographed. SS26 brings new eveningwear cut for galas, weddings and the kind of nights that end at sunrise. Find yours before the season begins.
concepts.visual_direction: Editorial grid of three real customer/celebrity moments in Mouret, each tagged with the event and dress name.
concepts.fills_gap: Introduces social proof and occasion-based intent — neither exists in current rotation.

unlocks: Cut duplicate spend across 2 ad pairs and reclaim ~40% of budget
Push days-until-fatigue from 9 to 25+ with 3 distinct angles in market
Open a hero-product lane (Galaxy) that compounds brand equity, not just clicks
Give Meta enough creative variance to actually optimize delivery

next_step.urgency: Ads #1 and #2 hit fatigue inside 7 days — replacements need to be in market by next Monday.
next_step.headline: Let's ship the SS26 rotation before CPMs lift
next_step.body: Book a 30-minute call and I'll walk you through how OmniRocket would produce and launch the three concepts above this week. You'll leave with a shot list, briefs, and a kill/scale plan ready to action.

method_note: Scores derived from creative-signal heuristics (duplication, copy specificity, format mix, hero-product presence) on the 4 live ads scraped from Meta Ad Library — not account-level delivery data.
